phrases.translation: And the LORD is pleased to crush him
phrases.summary: The verse states that the LORD wants (is pleased) to crush/afflict “him,” indicating the LORD’s active will or desire toward that action.

phrases.translation: If he makes his soul an offering for sin
phrases.summary: It describes a condition in which “he” gives up or offers his soul as a guilt/offering for sin, presenting his participation in making that offering.

phrases.translation: He will see seed and will prolong days
phrases.summary: Following the offering, the text asserts what “he” will experience: he will see descendants (“seed”) and live for a longer time (“prolong days”).

phrases.translation: And the LORD’s pleasure/will will prosper in his hand
phrases.summary: The verse concludes that the LORD’s will/pleasure will succeed, specifically “in his hand,” meaning his role/agency is associated with that successful fulfillment.
